label: 1202 Commercial Mobile Service Alert Advisory Committee

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: a Establishment
children.text: Not later than 60 days after , the chairman of the Federal Communications Commission shall establish an advisory committee, to be known as the Commercial Mobile Service Alert Advisory Committee (referred to in this section as the “Advisory Committee”).October 13, 2006

children.heading: State and local government representatives
children.label: 1 State and local government representatives
children.text: Representatives of State and local governments and representatives of emergency response providers, selected from among individuals nominated by national organizations representing such governments and personnel.

children.heading: Tribal governments
children.label: 2 Tribal governments
children.text: Representatives from Federally recognized Indian tribes and National Indian organizations.

children.label: A
children.text: communications service providers;

children.label: B
children.text: vendors, developers, and manufacturers of systems, facilities, equipment, and capabilities for the provision of communications services;

children.label: C
children.text: third-party service bureaus;

children.label: D
children.text: technical experts from the broadcasting industry;

children.label: E
children.text: the national organization representing the licensees and permittees of noncommercial broadcast television stations;

children.label: F
children.text: national organizations representing individuals with special needs, including individuals with disabilities and the elderly; and

children.label: G
children.text: other individuals with relevant technical expertise.

children.heading: Qualified representatives of other stakeholders and interested parties
children.label: 4 Qualified representatives of other stakeholders and interested parties
children.text: Qualified representatives of such other stakeholders and interested and affected parties as the chairman deems appropriate.
for protocols, technical capabilities, and technical procedures through which electing commercial mobile service providers receive, verify, and transmit alerts to subscribers;
for the establishment of technical standards for priority transmission of alerts by electing commercial mobile service providers to subscribers;
for relevant technical standards for devices and equipment and technologies used by electing commercial mobile service providers to transmit emergency alerts to subscribers;
for the technical capability to transmit emergency alerts by electing commercial mobile providers to subscribers in languages in addition to English, to the extent practicable and feasible;

under which electing commercial mobile service providers may offer subscribers the capability of preventing the subscriber’s device from receiving emergency alerts, or classes of such alerts, (other than an alert issued by the President), consistent with ;section 1201(b)(2)(E) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: not all of the devices or equipment used by such provider are capable of receiving such alerts; or

children.label: B
children.text: the provider cannot offer such alerts throughout the entirety of its service area; and
as otherwise necessary to enable electing commercial mobile service providers to transmit emergency alerts to subscribers.

children.heading: Initial meeting
children.label: 1 Initial meeting
children.text: The initial meeting of the Advisory Committee shall take place not later than 60 days after .October 13, 2006

children.heading: Other meetings
children.label: 2 Other meetings
children.text: After the initial meeting, the Advisory Committee shall meet at the call of the chair.

children.heading: Notice; open meetings
children.label: 3 Notice; open meetings
children.text: Any meetings held by the Advisory Committee shall be duly noticed at least 14 days in advance and shall be open to the public.

children.heading: Quorum
children.label: 1 Quorum
children.text: One-third of the members of the Advisory Committee shall constitute a quorum for conducting business of the Advisory Committee.

children.heading: Subcommittees
children.label: 2 Subcommittees
children.text: To assist the Advisory Committee in carrying out its functions, the chair may establish appropriate subcommittees composed of members of the Advisory Committee and other subject matter experts as deemed necessary.

children.heading: Additional rules
children.label: 3 Additional rules
children.text: The Advisory Committee may adopt other rules as needed.

children.heading: Chapter 10 of title 5
children.label: f Chapter 10 of title 5
children.text: Neither chapter 10 of title 5 nor any rule, order, or regulation promulgated under that chapter shall apply to the Advisory Committee.

children.heading: Consultation with NIST
children.label: g Consultation with NIST
children.text: The Advisory Committee shall consult with the National Institute of Standards and Technology in its work on developing recommendations under paragraphs (2) and (3) of subsection (c).
